name: Copier (Modern Project Template Scaffolding with Updates)

auth.notes: CLI tool; GitHub templates via HTTPS or SSH

performance.notes: Template rendering fast; network download from template repo adds latency

agent_readiness.notes: Modern Cookiecutter alternative with template update support. copier copy gh:org/template my-project. copier update (applies template changes to existing project via git diff). copier.yml for questions/variables. Jinja2 templating + Jinja2-based file names. copier.update() respects .copier-answers.yml for re-applying context. Python API: from copier import run_auto. Migration scripts: _migrations/ for breaking template changes. Answers stored in .copier-answers.yml. Key differentiator from Cookiecutter: update existing projects when template evolves.

gotchas: Requires git in the project for update functionality
Complex update conflicts when template and project both heavily modified
copier.yml syntax is different from Cookiecutter's cookiecutter.json
best_when: Projects that need to receive template updates as template evolves
Organization-wide project standardization where templates change over time
Replacing Cookiecutter when update propagation is needed
Infrastructure or CI/CD templates that need version management
avoid_when: One-off project generation (Cookiecutter simpler for this)
When the large Cookiecutter template ecosystem is needed
summary: Copier is the modern project template tool with update support — the key advantage over Cookiecutter. copier copy gh:org/template my-project. copier update propagates template changes to existing projects via git diff. copier.yml for questions. .copier-answers.yml stores context for updates. MIT. Best for organization templates that evolve over time. For one-shot generation, Cookiecutter has more community templates.
